title_en: The Revelation and Early Islam in Mecca
title_id: Wahyu dan Islam Awal di Mekah
content_id:
Pada usia empat puluh tahun, di bulan Ramadan, Muhammad ibn Abd Allah, semoga shalawat dan salam terlimpah atasnya, memiliki kebiasaan menyendiri di gua Hira di Jabal al-Nur — Gunung Cahaya — di luar kota Mekah. Ia telah lama dikenal di kalangan kaumnya sebagai al-Amin, orang yang dapat dipercaya, dan selama bertahun-tahun ia merasakan kegelisahan yang tak bernama, sebuah penolakan terhadap penyembahan berhala dan kekacauan moral di sekelilingnya. Di dalam gua itu, sendirian bersama perenungannya, wahyu pertama turun.

Malaikat Jibril muncul dan memerintahkan: 'Bacalah.' Nabi menjawab bahwa ia tidak bisa membaca. Malaikat memeluknya dengan kekuatan sedemikian rupa sehingga seolah-olah tenaga terperas darinya, lalu melepaskannya dan mengulang perintah itu. Ini terjadi tiga kali. Kemudian turunlah ayat-ayat pertama yang pernah diwahyukan dari al-Quran: 'Bacalah dengan nama Tuhanmu yang menciptakan. Dia telah menciptakan manusia dari segumpal darah. Bacalah, dan Tuhanmulah yang Maha Pemurah — yang mengajar manusia dengan pena, mengajar manusia apa yang tidak diketahuinya.'

Dengan gemetar dan tak menentu, Nabi turun dari gunung dan kembali kepada istrinya Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. Ia memintanya menyelimutinya dengan kain, tubuhnya menggigil. Ketika ia menceritakan apa yang terjadi, Khadijah tidak ragu-ragu. Responnya adalah salah satu momen paling menakjubkan dalam Sirah: 'Tidak pernah! Demi Allah, Dia tidak akan pernah mempermalukanmu. Engkau menjaga silaturahmi, engkau menanggung beban orang-orang lemah, engkau memberi nafkah kepada orang yang tidak punya, engkau memuliakan tamu, dan engkau mendukung orang yang menegakkan kebenaran.' Penegasannya sebelum klaim kenabian apa pun secara resmi dibuat didasarkan pada pengetahuannya tentang siapa dirinya.

Khadijah membawanya kepada sepupunya Waraqah ibn Nawfal, seorang cendekiawan Kristen yang mengenali deskripsi malaikat Jibril dan memberi tahu Nabi bahwa peran besar menantinya — meskipun Waraqah menyesal bahwa ia sudah terlalu tua untuk menyaksikannya berlangsung.

Muslim pertama datang dari lingkaran terdekat. Khadijah adalah yang pertama beriman. Kemudian Ali ibn Abi Thalib, sepupu muda Nabi yang tinggal di rumahnya. Kemudian Zaid ibn Haritsah, mantan budaknya yang telah dimerdekakan. Kemudian Abu Bakr al-Shiddiq, sahabat terpercayanya, yang penerimaannya segera membawa yang lain. Sirah menelusuri bagaimana kesaksian dan pengaruh pribadi Abu Bakr membawa Utsman ibn Affan, al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, dan Thalhah ibn Ubaydullah kepada Islam pada hari-hari paling awal itu.

Seiring komunitas berkembang, begitu pula perlawanan. Para pemimpin Quraisy melihat dalam pesan baru ini ancaman terhadap tatanan sosial mereka, otoritas keagamaan mereka, dan kepentingan ekonomi yang dibangun di sekitar Ka'bah. Penganiayaan dimulai — bukan terhadap mereka yang memiliki perlindungan suku, melainkan terhadap kaum lemah: para budak yang dimerdekakan, orang asing, dan orang miskin. Bilal ibn Rabah disiksa di atas pasir Mekah yang membara di bawah beban batu besar yang diletakkan di dadanya oleh tuannya Umayyah ibn Khalaf, terus mengulang 'Ahad, Ahad' — 'Satu, Satu.' Abu Bakr membelinya dan memerdekakannya.

Keluarga Yasir — Yasir, istrinya Sumayyah, dan putra mereka Ammar — termasuk mereka yang tidak memiliki perlindungan suku. Sumayyah bint Khayyat menjadi syahid pertama dalam Islam, dibunuh oleh Abu Jahl. Nabi hanya bisa berkata kepada mereka, ketika melewati penyiksaan mereka: 'Bersabarlah, wahai keluarga Yasir. Tempat pertemuan kalian adalah surga.'

Bab-bab Sirah masa Mekah mengungkapkan baik keindahan iman komunitas awal di bawah tekanan maupun brutalitas perlawanan yang dihadapinya — perlawanan yang pada akhirnya akan mendorong kaum Muslim mencari perlindungan di luar Arabia.
content_en:
At the age of forty, in the month of Ramadan, Muhammad ibn Abd Allah, peace be upon him, was in the habit of retreating to the cave of Hira on the Mountain of Light — Jabal al-Nur — outside Mecca. He had long been known among his people as al-Amin, the trustworthy one, and for years he had felt an unnamed restlessness, a turning away from the idol worship and moral disorder around him. In that cave, alone with his contemplations, the first revelation descended.

The angel Jibril appeared and commanded: 'Read.' The Prophet replied that he could not read. The angel embraced him with such force that it seemed the strength was pressed out of him, then released him and repeated the command. This happened three times. Then came the first verses ever revealed of the Quran: 'Read in the name of your Lord who created. Created man from a clinging substance. Read, and your Lord is the Most Generous — who taught by the pen, taught man what he did not know.'

Shaken and uncertain, the Prophet descended from the mountain and returned to his wife Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. He asked her to wrap him in a garment, trembling. When he described what had happened, she did not hesitate. Her response is one of the most remarkable moments in the seerah: 'Never! By Allah, He will never disgrace you. You maintain family ties, you bear the burdens of the weak, you earn for the destitute, you honor your guests, and you support those who uphold the truth.' Her affirmation before any formal claim to prophethood had been made was rooted in her knowledge of who he was.

Khadijah took him to her cousin Waraqah ibn Nawfal, a Christian scholar who recognized the description of the angel Jibril and told the Prophet that a great role awaited him — though Waraqah lamented that he would be too old to live to see it unfold.

The first Muslims came from the closest circles. Khadijah was the first to believe. Then Ali ibn Abi Talib, the Prophet's young cousin living in his household. Then Zayd ibn Harithah, his freed slave. Then Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, his trusted friend, whose acceptance quickly brought others. The seerah traces how Abu Bakr's personal testimony and influence led Uthman ibn Affan, al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and Talhah ibn Ubaydullah to Islam in those earliest days.

As the community grew, so did the opposition. The leaders of Quraysh saw in this new message a threat to their social order, their religious authority, and the economic interests built around the Ka'bah. Persecution began — not against those with tribal protection, but against the vulnerable: freed slaves, foreigners, the poor. Bilal ibn Rabah was tortured on the burning sands of Mecca under the weight of a boulder placed on his chest by his master Umayyah ibn Khalaf, repeating 'Ahad, Ahad' — 'One, One.' Abu Bakr purchased him and freed him.

The family of Yasir — Yasir, his wife Sumayyah, and their son Ammar — were among those with no tribal protection. Sumayyah bint Khayyat became the first martyr of Islam, killed by Abu Jahl. The Prophet could only say to them, as he passed by their torment: 'Be patient, O family of Yasir. Your meeting place is paradise.'

The seerah's Meccan chapters reveal both the beauty of the early community's faith under pressure and the brutality of the resistance it faced — a resistance that would ultimately push the Muslims to seek refuge beyond Arabia.
content_ar: At the age of forty, in the month of Ramadan, محمد ﷺ ibn Abd الله, ﷺ, was in the habit of retreating to the cave of Hira on the Mountain of Light — Jabal al-Nur — outside مكة المكرمة. He had long been known among his people as al-آمين, the trustworthy one, and for years he had felt an unnamed restlessness, a turning away from the idol worship and moral disorder around him. In that cave, alone with his contemplations, the أولاً الوحي descended. The angel جبريل ﷺ appeared and commanded: 'Read.' النبي ﷺ replied that he could not read. The angel embraced him with such force that it seemed the القوة was pressed out of him, then released him and repeated the command. This happened three times. Then came the أولاً الآيات ever revealed of the القرآن الكريم: 'Read in the name of your Lord who created. Created man from a clinging substance. Read, and your Lord is the Most Generous — who taught by the pen, taught man what he did not know.' Shaken and uncertain, النبي ﷺ descended from the mountain and returned to his الزوجة Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. He asked her to wrap him in a garment, trembling. عندما he described what had happened, she did not hesitate. Her response is one of the most remarkable moments in the seerah: 'Never! By الله, He will never disgrace you. You maintain الأسرة ties, you bear the burdens of the weak, you earn for the destitute, you الشرف your guests, and you support those who uphold the الحق.' Her affirmation قبل any formal claim to prophethood had been made was rooted in her العلم of who he was. Khadijah took him to her cousin Waraqah ibn Nawfal, a Christian scholar who recognized the description of the angel جبريل ﷺ and told النبي ﷺ that a great role awaited him — though Waraqah lamented that he would be أيضاً old to live to see it unfold. The أولاً المسلمون came from the closest circles. Khadijah was the أولاً to believe. Then علي ibn Abi Talib, النبي ﷺ's young cousin living in his household. Then Zayd ibn Harithah, his freed slave. Then أبو بكر al-Siddiq, his trusted friend, whose acceptance quickly brought others. The seerah traces how أبو بكر's personal testimony and influence led عثمان ibn Affan, al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, Abd الرحمن ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and Talhah ibn Ubaydullah to الإسلام in those earliest days. As the الجماعة grew, لذا did the opposition. The leaders of Quraysh saw in this new message a threat to their social order, their religious authority, and the economic interests built around the الكعبة المشرفة. Persecution began — not against those with tribal protection, but against the vulnerable: freed slaves, foreigners, the poor. Bilal ibn Rabah was tortured on the burning sands of مكة المكرمة under the weight of a boulder placed on his chest by his master Umayyah ibn Khalaf, repeating 'Ahad, Ahad' — 'One, One.' أبو بكر purchased him and freed him.
